records.ety: [Contr. fr. fantasy, OF. fantasie, fantaisie, F. fantaisie, L. phantasia, fr. Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ appearance, imagination, the power of perception and presentation in the mind, fr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ to make visible, to place before one's mind, fr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ to show; akin to <?/<?/<?/<?/, <?/<?/<?/, light, Skr. bhāto shine. Cf. Fantasy, Fantasia, Epiphany, Phantom.]

defs: The faculty by which the mind forms an image or a representation of anything perceived before; the power of combining and modifying such objects into new pictures or images; the power of readily and happily creating and recalling such objects for the purpose of amusement, wit, or embellishment; imagination.
quotes: In the soul Are many lesser faculties, that serve Reason as chief. Among these fancy next Her office holds.

defs: An image or representation of anything formed in the mind; conception; thought; idea; conceit.
quotes: How now, my lord ! why do you keep alone, Of sorriest fancies your companoins making ?

defs: An opinion or notion formed without much reflection; caprice; whim; impression.
quotes: I have always had a fancy that learning might be made a play and recreation to children.

defs: Inclination; liking, formed by caprice rather than reason; as, to strike one's fancy; hence, the object of inclination or liking.
quotes: To fit your fancies to your father's will.

defs: That which pleases or entertains the taste or caprice without much use or value.
quotes: London pride is a pretty fancy for borders.

defs: A sort of love song or light impromptu ballad.
quotes: At a great book sale in London, which had congregated all the fancy.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Fancied (?), p. pr. & vb. n. Fancying (<?/).]

defs: To figure to one's self; to believe or imagine something without proof.
quotes: If our search has reached no farther than simile and metaphor, we rather fancy than know.

defs: To form a conception of; to portray in the mind; to imagine.
quotes: He whom I fancy, but can ne'er express.

defs: To have a fancy for; to like; to be pleased with, particularly on account of external appearance or manners.
To believe without sufficient evidence; to imagine (something which is unreal).
quotes: He fancied he was welcome, because those arounde him were his kinsmen.

defs: Adapted to please the fancy or taste, especially when of high quality or unusually appealing; ornamental; as, fancy goods; fancy clothes.

quotes: This anxiety never degenerated into a monomania, like that which led his [Frederick the Great's] father to pay fancy prices for giants.
